articles.title: Hotter days ahead? IMD flags heatwave risk across North, Central India - Zee Business
articles.full_text:
Heatwave Alert Issued: The India Meteorological Department (IMD), under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, on Saturday, April 25, issued detailed heatwave guidance and advisories as several parts of the country continue to experience rising temperatures and above-normal conditions.
Temperatures Rise Across Regions
As per the latest assessment, maximum temperatures in many parts of northwest, central and peninsular India are currently ranging between 40°C to 44°C, with the highest temperature of 44.5°C recorded at Sri Ganganagar (Rajasthan). In several regions, temperatures have been observed to be markedly above normal by 5°C or more, indicating a developing heat stress scenario across parts of the country.
Add Zee Business as a Preferred Source Heatwave Likely in Multiple States
The IMD has indicated that heatwave conditions are very likely to develop in isolated pockets of Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh & Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan and central India over the coming days. At the same time, hot and humid conditions are expected over coastal and eastern regions, while warm night conditions are likely in parts of north India, which may further increase discomfort levels and health risks.
No Major Change in Northwest Temperatures Till April 27
The department has further projected that there will be no significant change in maximum temperatures over northwest India till 27th April, followed by a gradual fall, while central and other regions may witness a gradual rise and subsequent decline in temperatures during the forecast period.
Heatwave Safety Advisory Issued
In view of the prevailing and forecasted conditions, IMD has issued comprehensive heatwave safety guidelines and advised citizens to take necessary precautions. These include avoiding prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, especially during peak afternoon hours, staying adequately hydrated, wearing light and breathable clothing, and avoiding strenuous outdoor activities during high-temperature periods. Special care has been advised for vulnerable groups such as children, the elderly and individuals with pre-existing health conditions.
Heat Conditions Likely to Persist Over Next 7 Days
The IMD has emphasised that early awareness and preventive action are critical in reducing heat-related illnesses such as heat exhaustion and heat stroke. The IMD’s extended outlook indicates that heatwave conditions are likely to persist across multiple regions over the next 7 days, particularly in parts of:
- Northwest India (Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh)
- Central India (Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Vidarbha)
- Select southern regions (Kerala & Mahe)
In addition, hot and humid weather conditions are expected in coastal states, including Odisha, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh, while warm night conditions may continue in northern plains, compounding heat stress during nighttime.
Seasonal Outlook Flags Above-Normal Heatwave Days
According to IMD’s seasonal outlook for April to June 2026, above-normal heatwave days are likely over parts of east, central and northwest India and the southeast peninsula, underscoring the need for continued preparedness and mitigation measures during the ongoing hot weather season.
Public Advisory and Continued Monitoring
The IMD has made comprehensive heatwave guidance, including do’s and don’ts, preparedness measures and sector-specific advisories. Along with that, it continues to closely monitor temperature trends and issue timely forecasts and warnings, and citizens are advised to remain updated through official sources and adhere to the prescribed safety measures.

articles.title: Jammu sizzles at 39.5 deg C amid heatwave in plains - Greater Kashmir
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Jammu and Kashmir
articles.full_text:
Jammu, Apr 25: Jammu sweltered on Saturday as heatwave conditions gripped the plains, with the city recording a high of 39.5 degrees Celsius -- 5.1 notches above normal, officials said.
The minimum temperature in the city also remained above average at 22.8 degrees Celsius, 2.0 degrees above normal, a meteorological department spokesperson said.
Katra town, the base camp for pilgrims visiting the Mata Vaishno Devi shrine atop Trikuta hills in Reasi district, also witnessed a sharp rise in temperatures, recording a maximum of 36 degrees Celsius, 6.2 degrees above normal, while the minimum temperature settled at 23.0 degrees Celsius, 5.4 degrees above normal.
The persistent rise in both day and night temperatures has led to uncomfortable conditions, particularly during afternoon hours.
The Meteorological Department predicted that hot and dry weather with isolated heatwave conditions is likely to continue over the plains of Jammu division for the next four days.
Residents have been advised to take precautions, avoid prolonged sun exposure, and stay hydrated amid the prevailing sweltering conditions.

articles.title: Jammu Heatwave: Temperatures Hit 39.5°C Amid Sweltering Conditions - The Chenab Times
articles.full_text:
Jammu experienced sweltering conditions on Saturday as a heatwave intensified across the plains, with the city recording a high of 39.5 degrees Celsius. This temperature was significantly above the normal for this time of year, exceeding it by 5.1 notches, according to meteorological officials.
Information was available with The Chenab Times indicating that the minimum temperature in Jammu also remained elevated, settling at 22.8 degrees Celsius, which is 2.0 degrees above the seasonal average. These persistent high temperatures, both day and night, have contributed to uncomfortable living conditions, particularly during the afternoon hours.
The heatwave’s impact was also felt in Katra, a town serving as the base camp for pilgrims heading to the Mata Vaishno Devi shrine. Katra registered a maximum temperature of 36 degrees Celsius, a substantial 6.2 degrees above normal. The minimum temperature in Katra was recorded at 23.0 degrees Celsius, also well above average by 5.4 degrees.
The India Meteorological Department has forecast that the prevailing hot and dry weather conditions, including isolated heatwave occurrences, are expected to continue over the plains of the Jammu division for the next four days. Officials have issued advisories urging residents to take necessary precautions to mitigate the effects of the extreme heat.

articles.title: Jammu-Kashmir Weather : जम्मू-कश्मीर में रूक-रूक हो रही बारिश से मौसम सुहाना, गर्मी से बेहाल लोगों को मिली राहत - Dainik Tribune
articles.full_text:
Jammu-Kashmir Weather : कश्मीर के अधिकतर हिस्सों में शनिवार को रुक-रुक कर बारिश हुई, जिससे लोगों को लू से राहत मिली। अधिकारियों ने कहा कि मौसम विभाग ने अगले सप्ताह के शुरु में एक बार फिर बारिश होने का अनुमान लगाया है। ग्रीष्मकालीन राजधानी श्रीनगर समेत कश्मीर के मैदानी इलाकों में शनिवार सुबह तक लगातार बारिश होती रही। जोजिला दर्रे में हल्की बर्फबारी की भी खबर है।
दोपहर में मौसम में सुधार आया। बारिश के चलते तापमान में गिरावट आयी है जिससे पिछले कुछ दिनों से घाटी में व्याप्त लू से लोगों को राहत मिली। श्रीनगर में शुक्रवार को अधिकतम तापमान 28.1 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया, जो सामान्य से छह डिग्री से अधिक था। न्यूनतम तापमान भी औसत से लगभग पांच डिग्री अधिक रहा।
सामान्य तौर पर, शुक्रवार को कश्मीर के अधिकतर स्थानों पर तापमान सामान्य से छह डिग्री अधिक था। अधिकारियों ने बताया कि हालांकि, शनिवार को बारिश और बादल छाए रहने के कारण तापमान गिरकर 21.2 डिग्री सेल्सियस हो गया - जो पिछले दिन की तुलना में लगभग सात डिग्री कम और औसत से लगभग एक डिग्री कम था। घाटी के सभी मौसम केंद्रों ने शनिवार को सामान्य से कम दिन का तापमान दर्ज किया।
मौसम विभाग ने कहा कि रविवार को मौसम आमतौर पर शुष्क रहने की संभावना है, लेकिन सोमवार से छिटपुट इलाकों में हल्की बारिश, गरज के साथ बौछारें और तेज हवाएं चलने की आशंका है। घाटी में 29-30 अप्रैल की दोपहर बाद कुछ स्थानों पर गरज के साथ बौछारें पड़ने की संभावना है।
